skill.name: clinvar-database
skill.description: Utilities for querying the NCBI ClinVar database to retrieve variant records, clinical significance, and phenotype relationships; use when searching variants by gene/condition/significance, interpreting Pathogenic/Benign/VUS classifications, or annotating VCF files with ClinVar annotations.
skill.summary: Query the NCBI ClinVar database for variant records, clinical significance, and VCF annotation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged 49 potential issues, including 10 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings and numerous shell backtick execution detections. All high-severity findings are false positives triggered by numeric values in audit JSON data and descriptive text in markdown documentation, not by any actual cryptographic operations. The confirmed findings are limited to legitimate subprocess execution of bcftools for VCF annotation and documentation references to public NCBI APIs and FTP, which are standard and expected patterns for this bioinformatics skill. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential harvesting, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for VCF Annotation
medium_findings.description: scripts/annotate.py uses subprocess.run to execute the bcftools annotate command. This is a legitimate bioinformatics workflow pattern. The command arguments are hardcoded list elements, not a shell string, which prevents command injection. The bcftools tool is a standard dependency documented in SKILL.md. This is considered acceptable risk for a bioinformatics annotation skill.

medium_findings.title: FTP Protocol Reference in Documentation
medium_findings.description: references/api_reference.md documents the public NCBI ClinVar FTP URL (ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pub/clinvar/). This is standard documentation for researchers who need to download bulk ClinVar datasets. The FTP reference is not an active connection from the skill itself - it is informational documentation for the user to perform manual downloads.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 10 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across 4 files. All are false positives. The audit JSON file (clinvar-database_audit_result_v1.json) lines 47, 52, 72, 77 contain performance scores and audit notes, not cryptographic operations. Scripts annotate.py:41 and search.py:40 contain ArgumentParser declarations. SKILL.md:3 and 62 contain YAML frontmatter and pipeline documentation text. No cryptographic algorithms are used anywhere in this skill.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 33 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in references/api_reference.md and SKILL.md. All instances are within markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) showing example commands for users to copy and run manually. SKILL.md contains example bash commands for running scripts/search.py and scripts/annotate.py. api_reference.md documents API endpoints and FTP URLs in inline code formatting. These are documentation artifacts, not executable code within the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Network URLs (Legitimate Public APIs)
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged hardcoded URLs across multiple files. scripts/search.py uses https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi which is the official NCBI E-utilities API endpoint for ClinVar searches. SKILL.md and api_reference.md reference the NCBI GitHub repository and FTP server for documentation. All URLs point to legitimate public NCBI/ government resources and are expected for a ClinVar database query skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positives in Audit JSON
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged two 'system reconnaissance' patterns at clinvar-database_audit_result_v1.json:23 and 225. Line 23 contains audit review text: 'The legacy audit did not indicate that retrieval outputs were presented as unsupported findings.' Line 225 notes: 'The archived evaluation did not see this scenario drift outside the declared scope.' Neither of these constitutes system reconnaissance. The pattern match was likely triggered by generic terms like 'findings' or 'scope' in the text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Execution for External Tool
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/annotate.py:31 calls subprocess.run to execute the bcftools command-line tool. While this is a legitimate pattern for a bioinformatics annotation skill, the execution of external system commands always carries inherent risk. In this implementation, the command is constructed as a list (no shell=True), which prevents shell injection. Input and output file paths come from command-line arguments. The skill depends on bcftools being installed on the system.

content.user_title: Query ClinVar Variants and Annotate Genomic Data
content.value_statement: Researchers and clinicians struggle to efficiently retrieve variant interpretations from the NCBI ClinVar database. This skill provides ready-to-use scripts for searching ClinVar by gene or condition and annotating VCF files with clinical significance data.
actual_capabilities: Search ClinVar for variant records using gene names, conditions, or clinical significance
Retrieve clinical significance classifications such as Pathogenic, Benign, and VUS with review status
Annotate VCF files with ClinVar variant identifiers and interpretation data
Access NCBI E-utilities API for programmatic ClinVar queries
Reference documentation for ClinVar API endpoints and clinical significance definitions
Support bulk data retrieval via NCBI FTP for offline processing workflows
limitations: Requires bcftools and the Python requests library to be installed in the execution environment
Does not include built-in VCF parsing or variant normalization logic
Subject to NCBI E-utilities API rate limits during query operations
Bulk data retrieval requires manual FTP download of large ClinVar dataset files

use_cases.title: Clinical Variant Research
use_cases.description: A researcher searches ClinVar for pathogenic variants in a specific gene to identify known disease-associated mutations for a literature review or study design.

use_cases.title: VCF Annotation Pipeline
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician integrates ClinVar annotation into a batch VCF processing pipeline for variant interpretation in a diagnostic laboratory setting.

use_cases.title: Variant Classification Education
use_cases.description: A medical genetics educator demonstrates how ClinVar assigns clinical significance categories using real database queries as teaching examples.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Gene Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ClinVar for variants in the [GENE_NAME] gene that are classified as pathogenic. Show me the count and IDs of matching records.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to find disease-associated variants for a specific gene.

prompt_templates.title: Annotate a VCF File
prompt_templates.prompt: Annotate the VCF file at [INPUT_PATH] with ClinVar data and save the result to [OUTPUT_PATH]. Use the annotate.py script with default settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a VCF file from sequencing and want to add ClinVar annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret Clinical Significance
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the clinical significance categories for variant IDs [VARIANT_IDS] from ClinVar. Explain what Pathogenic, Benign, and VUS mean for these results.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to understand the clinical relevance of specific genomic variants.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Download ClinVar Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the latest ClinVar VCF files for GRCh38 from the NCBI FTP server. Outline the steps to set up a local ClinVar annotation resource.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to set up a local ClinVar database for high-throughput batch processing.

output_examples.input: Search ClinVar for pathogenic variants in BRCA1.
output_examples.output: Found 15 pathogenic variants in BRCA1. The results include variant IDs, clinical significance (Pathogenic), and review status. Each variant can be further inspected using its unique ClinVar accession number.

output_examples.input: What does VUS mean in ClinVar and how should I interpret it in a clinical report?
output_examples.output: VUS stands for Variant of Uncertain Significance. This means there is insufficient evidence to determine if the variant is disease-causing or benign. In clinical reports, VUS results should not be used for clinical decision-making. Further family studies or functional evidence may reclassify a VUS over time.

output_examples.input: Annotate my sample.vcf with ClinVar data for cancer gene panel analysis.
output_examples.output: The annotation process uses bcftools to add ClinVar IDs and interpretation fields to each variant record. The tool expects bcftools to be installed. After annotation, your VCF will include ClinVar accession numbers and clinical significance for variants that have ClinVar records.

best_practices: Always specify the gene and clinical significance in search terms for focused results, for example BRCA1[gene] AND pathogenic[CLNSIG].
Verify that bcftools is installed and accessible in your PATH before running annotation workflows.
Use bulk FTP downloads for large-scale or repeated analyses to avoid exceeding NCBI API rate limits.
anti_patterns: Do not use ClinVar results as the sole basis for clinical diagnosis without reviewing primary literature and local population data.
Do not assume VUS (Variant of Uncertain Significance) is benign or pathogenic without additional functional evidence.
Do not run annotation scripts on VCF files without confirming they are properly formatted and normalized.

faq.question: What is ClinVar?
faq.answer: ClinVar is a public database from NCBI that archives human genetic variants and their relationship to disease, with supporting evidence.

faq.question: What clinical significance categories does ClinVar use?
faq.answer: ClinVar categories include Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Variant of Uncertain Significance (VUS), Likely Benign, and Benign.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use NCBI E-utilities?
faq.answer: No, an API key is not required. However, providing one allows higher request rate limits of 10 requests per second instead of 3.

faq.question: What is a VCF file?
faq.answer: VCF (Variant Call Format) is a standard file format for storing genetic sequence variations. It is widely used in bioinformatics.

faq.question: Can this skill make clinical recommendations?
faq.answer: No. This skill retrieves ClinVar records and annotations for research and educational purposes. It does not provide clinical advice.

faq.question: How do I install bcftools?
faq.answer: Install bcftools through your package manager, such as apt install bcftools or brew install bcftools, or download from the samtools website.
